basics.name: Mike Blom
basics.tagline: Director of Engineering at Instructure — a leader delivering high-confidence roadmaps for multi-team, multi-year platform initiatives.
basics.summary: Born a US citizen in the Netherlands, I grew up working every odd job I could find, then moved to the United States alone after high school. I spent over a decade serving with the US Army and Department of State in combat zones around the world, taught myself to write software in 2014, and have spent the years since in education technology — from self-taught engineer at MasteryConnect to Director of Engineering at Instructure, the makers of Canvas. Known for turning ambiguity into clear plans, shipping reliably at scale, and fostering a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
basics.philosophy: I lead talented and passionate engineering teams who build and maintain innovative, scalable web applications for online education. My military and government service built foundational skills in communication, de-escalation, reliability, leadership, and ownership — and I apply them daily: overseeing engineering processes, standards, and best practices; ensuring the quality, performance, and security of our products; and mentoring and coaching my team members while fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. I take pride in delivering intelligent, optimized backends behind smooth, fast frontends. My mission is to empower educators and learners with the tools they need to succeed in the digital age.

chapters.title: Youth in the Netherlands
chapters.summary: Born a US citizen in the Netherlands. A paper route, stocking grocery shelves, making orthopedic shoes, cleaning homes — every little job taught responsibility, independence, and the habit of showing up.

chapters.title: US Army & Department of State
chapters.summary: After high school, moved to the United States alone to start a new life. More than a decade of military and government service in combat zones around the world: leadership, communication, de-escalation, and calm execution in high-risk environments.

chapters.title: Software Engineering & EdTech Leadership
chapters.summary: Taught myself to write software in 2014 and joined MasteryConnect, growing from engineer to Team Lead through consistent delivery. Continued through acquisition into Instructure, where I now lead engineering teams as Director of Engineering.

chapters.title: The Unwritten Future
chapters.summary: The next chapter isn't written yet. By now you know who I am, what I've done, and how I lead — let's talk about what comes next.

experience.title: Director of Engineering
experience.summary: Leading distributed international engineering teams building scalable web applications for online education.
highlights: Led assessment platform unification, aligning Product, Design, and Engineering on one roadmap
Scaled distributed international teams, increasing ownership, autonomy, and predictable execution
Cut projected engineering effort ~70% using high-confidence planning and explicit trade-offs

experience.title: Engineering Team Lead
experience.summary: Self-taught engineer who earned a seat through delivery, then kept delivering.
highlights: Highest-volume contributor across 100+ engineers, shipping core production capabilities
Fast-tracked to Senior Engineer and Team Lead through consistent delivery
Scaled architecture and features supporting growth through acquisition

military.branch: US Army & US Department of State
military.summary: More than ten years in combat zones around the world. A trusted leader in high-risk environments requiring discretion, judgment, and calm execution — safeguarding people, assets, and reputations through communication and de-escalation.

stories.title: The Paper Route
stories.body: My first real job: delivering newspapers through the Dutch streets — rain, wind, or shine, the papers went out. Reliability, timing, and the habit of showing up every single day started here. A playable Paper Route mini-game is coming to this doorway in a future build.

stories.title: Every Odd Job I Could Find
stories.body: Stocking grocery shelves. Making orthopedic shoes. Cleaning homes for the elderly. None of it glamorous — all of it teaching the same lesson: work is work, do it well, and people will trust you with more.

stories.title: 2003 — Leaving for America
stories.body: Born a US citizen in the Netherlands, I graduated high school and wanted a change — so I left my family behind and crossed the ocean to start a new life. What followed was more than a decade of service with the US Army and Department of State in combat zones around the world. That chapter of this world is still under construction.

stories.title: 2014 — Teaching Myself to Code
stories.body: After returning to civilian life, I taught myself to write software and earned a seat at MasteryConnect through sheer delivery — then kept delivering, all the way through acquisition into Instructure.

stories.title: The Unwritten Future
stories.body: You've walked through where I come from and what I've built. The next chapter isn't written yet — if you're building something worth building, let's talk. Find me on LinkedIn, browse the code behind this world on GitHub, or grab the traditional resume from the clouds above.

skills.name: Engineering
items: Ruby on Rails, JavaScript/TypeScript, Node, Nest, Go, React, Angular
